target.title: fix(agent): classify stuck recovery as idle timeout
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Fixes #93610. opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash runs that stopped making model-stream progress were recovered by the stuck-session watchdog through the same active-run abort path used for user/restart/superseded aborts. That marked the attempt as `externalAbort=true`, so embedded-run failover surfaced `Request was aborted` even when fallback was configured.
- Solution: Preserve the diagnostic `stuck_recovery` abort reason, then classify the active attempt phase before deciding whether retry/fallback is replay-safe.
- What changed: `stuck_recovery` now becomes an internal model idle timeout only after a model call has started and no active potential-side-effect tool execution is in flight. Active side-effecting tool stalls become tool-execution timeouts, which stay out of automatic retry/fallback paths. Unknown/pre-model recovery remains external-abort-like instead of being replayed. The tool handler now tracks active potential-side-effect tool executions, and both diagnostic active-run abort paths preserve `abortReason: "stuck_recovery"`.
- What did NOT change: No provider catalog changes, no OpenAI-compatible stream parser changes, no opencode-go payload wrapper changes, no fallback policy rewrite, and no gateway protocol/config changes.
- Why it matters / User impact: Cron/provider sessions that stall during model streaming can still reuse the existing idle-timeout rotate/fallback path, while stuck side-effecting tools are not automatically replayed.
- Fixes #93610

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:**
```text
Stuck diagnostic recovery now preserves a distinct `stuck_recovery` abort reason and classifies the active attempt before failover observes it:
- model-call stall with no active potential-side-effect tool => model idle timeout, eligible for the existing rotate/fallback path;
- active side-effecting tool stall => tool-execution timeout, not idle timeout, so failover does not replay the tool call;
- unknown/pre-model stuck recovery => external-abort-like path, not a replay path.
```

- **Real environment tested:**
```text
Local OpenClaw existing-PR worktree: /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-93655
HEAD: 74eb3d5564772110a7d59c2a2151f7ad9086333e
Environment: Linux with frozen pnpm dependencies installed in the PR worktree.
```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```sh
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/stuck-recovery-abort.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:test:src
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
```

- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 40.78s
Test Files  2 passed (2) / Tests 34 passed (34)
Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests 22 passed (22)
Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests 69 passed (69)

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo
```

Evidence logs:
```text
/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93655-evidence/vitest-phase-safe-pr-head.log
/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93655-evidence/typecheck-core-phase-safe-pr-head.log
/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93655-evidence/typecheck-test-src-phase-safe-pr-head.log
/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93655-evidence/diff-check-phase-safe-pr-head.log
/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93655-evidence/verification-marker.json
```

- **Observed result after fix:**
```text
`classifyStuckRecoveryAbort` proves model-call stalls map to `model_idle_timeout`, side-effecting tool-call stalls map to `tool_execution_timeout`, and pre-model/unknown recovery maps to `external_abort`.
`embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts` proves an active `write` tool is counted as a potential side effect until tool end, while a replay-safe `read` tool is not counted as side-effecting.
`diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts` proves both active-run recovery paths preserve `abortReason: "stuck_recovery"`.
`failover-policy.test.ts` keeps the existing distinction: LLM idle timeouts can rotate/fallback, while tool-execution timeouts do not rotate/fallback.
`git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` produced no output.
```

- **What was not tested:**
```text
I did not run a live opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash provider stream or a live cron job against opencode.ai. The proof is local source/runtime coverage of the abort-classification boundary that produced `externalAbort=true` in the issue trace, plus regression coverage for the maintainer-requested side-effecting tool-call stall boundary.
```

- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** ClawSweeper reported that review did not complete, so this update provides current-head proof and an explicit maintainer-ready explanation rather than relying on the earlier body.
- **RF-002:** Maintainer feedback on #93655: mapping every stuck recovery to idle timeout loses the distinction between stalled model calls and stalled tool calls, and retrying the latter can replay side effects. Addressed by classifying stuck recovery before abort semantics are set: only model-call stalls without active potential-side-effect tools become idle timeouts; active side-effecting tool stalls become tool-execution timeouts and stay out of fallback/retry.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/stuck-recovery-abort.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts`, `src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts`, and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** Diagnostic stuck recovery carries `abortReason: "stuck_recovery"`; model-call stalls are replay-safe idle timeouts only when no active potential-side-effect tool execution exists; side-effecting tool stalls are classified as tool-execution timeouts and do not trigger automatic fallback/retry.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The bug is at the abort-classification boundary between diagnostic recovery and embedded attempt failover, not in provider-specific SSE parsing. The tests cover the classification helper, the active tool side-effect signal, diagnostic reason plumbing, and the existing failover decision table.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** availability / automation / session-state / side-effect replay.
- **Risk explanation:** The change touches active embedded-run abort classification. Incorrect classification could affect session cleanup, user aborts, restart aborts, timeout retry/fallback behavior, or side-effecting tool replay safety.
- **Why acceptable:** The diff preserves user/restart/superseded abort behavior, does not relax fallback policy, uses active tool side-effect state already derived from tool replay safety, keeps force-clear cleanup unchanged, and is covered by targeted runtime tests, failover-policy tests, type checks, and diff checks.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The root/source defect was an abort-classification contract violation between diagnostic stuck-session recovery and embedded attempt failover. `recoverStuckDiagnosticSession` knew it was reclaiming a stale internal run (`reason: "stuck_recovery"`), but the original registry path discarded that reason and the first patch then mapped every recovered stuck run to `idleTimedOut=true`. That fixed the model-stream stall trace but was too broad: a stuck side-effecting tool call is not replay-safe, so classifying it as idle model silence could trigger retry/fallback and replay side effects.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** This fixes the owner boundary where replay semantics must be decided. Diagnostic recovery still preserves `abortReason: "stuck_recovery"`, but the active attempt now checks whether a model call has started and whether any active potential-side-effect tool execution exists before choosing abort semantics. The existing failover policy remains the source of truth for whether model idle timeouts rotate/fallback and for keeping tool-execution timeouts out of replay paths.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** Focused runner-state fix with regression coverage; no provider-specific special case, no parser fallback, no scope expansion, and no broad failover-policy relaxation.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Source-of-truth ownership stays in the embedded run registry (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runs.ts` / `run-state.ts`), diagnostic recovery (`src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.ts`), attempt-local stuck-recovery classification (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/stuck-recovery-abort.ts`), and active tool replay-safety state (`src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts`). This changes abort semantics before failover observes them, not downstream PR/body/log formatting.

## Public contract review

- **Contract surface:** Internal embedded-run abort reason plumbing and attempt-local recovery classification only.
- **Compatibility / default behavior:** No public API, protocol, config, schema, provider catalog, model default, or gateway behavior changes. Existing `user_abort`, `restart`, and `superseded` semantics remain external aborts. Existing failover policy remains unchanged.
- **Related open PR / competition scan:** Current submission recheck found linked open PR #93640. That PR adds an OpenAI transport guard that only fires after a chunk with `finish_reason`; this submission fixes the failover boundary explicitly shown in the issue trace (`model.completed` with `aborted=true`, `externalAbort=true`, `fallbackConfigured=true`, `decision=surface_error`) and now also addresses maintainer feedback by preventing side-effecting tool-call stalls from being mapped to idle model timeouts. The gap matters because the issue impact is not just a missing stream terminator: once diagnostic stuck-session recovery aborts the active embedded run, the registry/attempt boundary must preserve the internal reason and classify replay safety before failover. This PR keeps the fix provider-agnostic, without changing OpenAI stream parsing or opencode-go model configuration.
- **Out of scope:** Live provider transport behavior, opencode-go SSE parsing, fallback model selection policy, cron scheduler timeout policy, and broader tool replay-policy changes are intentionally unchanged.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
